content: Just as the common language definition of reflexion encompasses both "reflection of light/information off a surface" and intentional deliberative reflection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate these two core functions: a passive reflexion step that bounces the LLM's own output off a fixed implicit knowledge boundary to detect inconsistencies, and an active reflexion step that deliberate revises flawed reasoning. This dual design draws directly from the dual meaning of the original term that has been implicit in language long before AI agents were developed.
